item.title: IDFC First Bank Ltd Falls 5.08%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: IDFC First Bank Ltd experienced a challenging week from 11 to 15 May 2026, with its share price declining by 5.08% to close at Rs.67.64, underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63% over the same period. The week was marked by a notable downgrade in valuation attractiveness, a shift in mojo grade to Sell, and heavy trading volumes amid bearish momentum, reflecting a cautious market stance on the mid-cap private sector bank. Time: 16 minutes ago

item.title: Advanced Enzyme Technologies Ltd Gains 1.86%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Advanced Enzyme Technologies Ltd closed the week with a modest gain of 1.86%, ending at Rs.363.95 on 15 May 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 2.63% over the same period. The week was marked by a remarkable surge on 11 May, when the stock hit a new 52-week high of Rs.412.5 amid strong technical momentum and sector outperformance, followed by a gradual correction and consolidation in subsequent sessions. Time: 16 minutes ago

item.title: Alkem Laboratories Declines 1.64% Amid Valuation Shift and Downgrade
item.description: Alkem Laboratories Ltd closed the week ending 15 May 2026 at Rs.5,485.25, down 1.64% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.5,576.80. This decline came despite the broader Sensex falling 2.63% over the same period, indicating a relative outperformance by Alkem. The week was marked by a valuation downgrade to a fair grade and a subsequent rating cut to Sell by MarketsMOJO, reflecting growing concerns over stretched multiples and subdued financial trends amid a volatile market backdrop. Time: 16 minutes ago

item.title: Agribio Spirits Ltd Falls 1.37%: Key Factors Behind the Weekly Decline
item.description: Agribio Spirits Ltd experienced a modest decline of 1.37% over the week ending 15 May 2026, closing at Rs.299.45 from Rs.303.60. This performance contrasted with the broader Sensex, which fell by 2.63% during the same period, indicating relative resilience amid a challenging market backdrop. The week was marked by the stock reaching new 52-week and all-time highs on 11 May, followed by a period of consolidation and mixed trading activity. Time: 16 minutes ago

item.title: GNA Axles Ltd Falls 8.30%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: GNA Axles Ltd experienced a challenging week, with its stock declining 8.30% from Rs.404.85 to Rs.371.25, underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63% over the same period. Despite posting record quarterly revenue and strong profit growth, the stock faced technical headwinds and a valuation recalibration amid broader market volatility. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: KIOCL Ltd Falls 3.41%: 5 Key Technical and Fundamental Factors Driving the Week
item.description: KIOCL Ltd’s stock closed the week at Rs.387.95, down 3.41% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.401.65, marginally underperforming the Sensex’s 2.63% decline over the same period. The week was marked by a series of technical momentum shifts and a significant downgrade to a Strong Sell rating by MarketsMOJO, reflecting mixed financial signals and uncertain near-term price direction amid volatile market conditions. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: LTM Ltd Falls 8.77%: 3 Key Factors Behind the Steep Weekly Decline
item.description: LTM Ltd’s shares declined sharply over the week ending 15 May 2026, falling 8.77% from Rs.4,350.60 to Rs.3,969.20, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 2.63% drop. The stock faced sustained selling pressure amid broad market weakness and sector-specific challenges, hitting a fresh 52-week low and recording notable intraday lows on multiple days. Despite the price decline, the company’s underlying financials remain robust, though technical indicators signal continued bearish momentum. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: K.P. Energy Ltd Falls 13.82%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: K.P. Energy Ltd endured a challenging week from 11 to 15 May 2026, with its share price declining by 13.82% to close at Rs.349.25, significantly underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63% over the same period. The stock’s volatility was marked by sharp daily losses early in the week, a brief midweek recovery, and renewed selling pressure towards the close. This review analyses the key technical shifts, valuation changes, and market dynamics that shaped the stock’s performance during this period. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Matrimony.com Ltd Falls 3.22%: Valuation Concerns and Quarterly Turnaround Shape Week
item.description: Matrimony.com Ltd’s shares declined by 3.22% over the week ending 15 May 2026, closing at ₹430.20 from ₹444.50, slightly underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63%. The week was marked by valuation concerns, a strong quarterly earnings turnaround, and mixed investor sentiment reflected in volatile daily price movements. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Narayana Hrudayalaya Gains 1.06%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Narayana Hrudayalaya Ltd recorded a modest gain of 1.06% over the week ending 15 May 2026, closing at Rs.1,838.95, outperforming the Sensex which declined 2.63% during the same period. The stock’s performance was shaped by a significant upgrade in its technical rating and a complex shift in momentum indicators, reflecting cautious optimism amid mixed financial signals and broader market volatility. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Parag Milk Foods Declines 1.58%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Parag Milk Foods Ltd closed the week at Rs.218.10, down 1.58% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.221.60, underperforming the Sensex which declined 2.63% over the same period. The stock experienced a volatile week marked by a mild bearish technical shift, valuation recalibration, and a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating, reflecting mixed signals amid broader market volatility. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Precision Camshafts Ltd Falls 7.88%: Mixed Technical Signals and Valuation Pressures Shape the Week
item.description: Precision Camshafts Ltd ended the week down 7.88% to close at Rs.155.45, underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63% over the same period. The stock’s volatile week featured a sharp intraday low on 12 May amid sectoral and market pressures, a shift in technical momentum to a mildly bearish stance, and a valuation upgrade to very expensive despite mixed returns. These developments collectively shaped a cautious outlook for the small-cap auto components firm. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Quick Heal Technologies Ltd Falls 2.33%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Quick Heal Technologies Ltd closed the week ending 15 May 2026 at Rs.209.25, down 2.33% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.214.25. Despite this decline, the stock outperformed the Sensex, which fell 2.63% over the same period. The week was marked by significant intraday volatility, exceptional trading volumes, and a notable gap up on 11 May, reflecting a complex interplay of strong short-term momentum and cautious longer-term sentiment. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Sayaji Industries Ltd Gains 15.47%: 8 Key Factors Driving the Surge
item.description: Sayaji Industries Ltd delivered a remarkable weekly performance from 11 to 15 May 2026, surging 15.47% to close at Rs.133.32, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 2.63% over the same period. The stock hit multiple new 52-week and all-time highs during the week, reflecting strong momentum amid a broadly cautious market environment. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Team Lease Services Ltd Gains 1.33%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Team Lease Services Ltd recorded a modest gain of 1.33% over the week ending 15 May 2026, closing at Rs.1,368.70 compared to Rs.1,350.70 the previous Friday. This performance notably outpaced the Sensex, which declined by 2.63% during the same period, reflecting relative resilience amid broader market weakness. The week was marked by a valuation reassessment and a significant gap up on 12 May, both influencing investor sentiment and price action. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Zim Laboratories Declines 3.98% Despite Technical Bullish Signals: 4 Key Events This Week
item.description: Zim Laboratories Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing at Rs.98.90 on 15 May 2026, down 3.98% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.103.00. Despite outperforming the Sensex on Monday with a strong 5.96% gain, the stock faced significant selling pressure midweek, culminating in a lower circuit hit on 14 May. The Sensex declined 2.63% over the same period, indicating that Zim Laboratories underperformed the broader market by 1.35 percentage points during the week. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Crompton Greaves Consumer Electricals Ltd Gains 2.81%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Crompton Greaves Consumer Electricals Ltd delivered a resilient performance during the week ending 15 May 2026, rising 2.81% to close at Rs.301.60, in stark contrast to the Sensex which declined 2.63% over the same period. The stock’s trajectory was shaped by valuation concerns early in the week, a mixed quarterly result midweek, and a strong intraday surge on the final trading day, reflecting a complex interplay of market sentiment and operational developments. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Bharat Wire Ropes Falls 8.76%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Slide
item.description: Bharat Wire Ropes Ltd experienced a turbulent week ending 15 May 2026, with its share price declining by 8.76% from Rs.250.70 to Rs.228.75, underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63% over the same period. Despite a strong start marked by a new 52-week high on 11 May, the stock faced sharp corrections amid volatile market conditions and mixed financial signals. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Bajaj Healthcare Declines 15.42%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Steep Weekly Drop
item.description: Bajaj Healthcare Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 11 to 15 May 2026, with its stock price plunging 15.42% to close at Rs.284.95, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 2.63% decline over the same period. The week was marked by a sharp downgrade to Strong Sell, intensified bearish technical momentum, a partial rating upgrade, and persistent fundamental challenges, all contributing to heightened volatility and investor caution. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Raghav Productivity Enhancers Ltd Gains 4.45%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Raghav Productivity Enhancers Ltd closed the week with a 4.45% gain, rising from Rs. 790.40 on 11 May to Rs. 825.60 on 15 May 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 2.63% over the same period. The stock experienced notable volatility, including a sharp rebound midweek following a technical momentum shift and a strong intraday surge, reflecting a complex but cautiously optimistic market sentiment. Time: 18 minutes ago
